task_completion.description: Verify that the sales_data.csv file has been loaded into Power BI.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Data' or 'Fields' pane on the right side of the Power BI interface shows a table named 'sales_data' with its respective columns.

task_completion.description: Verify that the required DAX measures (Total_Sales and Avg_Order_Value) have been created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 'Data' or 'Fields' pane, there are fields named 'Total_Sales' and 'Avg_Order_Value' with a small calculator icon next to them, indicating they are measures.

task_completion.description: Verify that the report page has been renamed to 'Sales Drivers'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The page tab at the bottom of the Power BI canvas area reads 'Sales Drivers' instead of 'Page 1'.

task_completion.description: Verify that a Multi-Row Card visual is present and displays the correct measures.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Multi-Row Card visual is visible on the report canvas displaying the calculated values for 'Total_Sales' and 'Avg_Order_Value'.

task_completion.description: Verify that a Decomposition Tree visual is present, analyzing Total_Sales by the required fields.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Decomposition Tree visual is visible on the canvas. It should show a root node for 'Total_Sales' with branches/plus-icons indicating it can be expanded (or is already expanded) by 'Region', 'Product_Category', 'Customer_Type', and 'Sales_Channel'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the report is saved with the correct name.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The title bar at the top of the Power BI Desktop window displays 'Sales_Decomposition' or 'Sales_Decomposition.pbix', or the file is visible on the Desktop.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Power BI Desktop GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the actual Power BI Desktop application interface being manipulated, not just a terminal, script editor, or text file.

integrity.description: Verify the visuals are genuinely rendered by Power BI.
integrity.visual_evidence: The visuals (Decomposition Tree and Multi-Row Card) should look like standard Power BI interactive elements with proper formatting, tooltips, or selection handles, rather than pasted static images or text boxes.

integrity.description: Verify the visuals contain actual data from the CSV.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Multi-Row Card and Decomposition Tree should display actual numerical values and category names (e.g., East, Clothing, New, Online) derived from the dataset, not placeholder text.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target file must be named 'Sales_Decomposition.pbix'. The report page must be renamed to 'Sales Drivers'. The model must contain two DAX measures: 'Total_Sales' and 'Avg_Order_Value'. The canvas must include a Multi-Row Card showing these two measures, and a Decomposition Tree analyzing 'Total_Sales' with the 'Explain by' fields set to 'Region', 'Product_Category', 'Customer_Type', and 'Sales_Channel'.
